content: I'm really struggling with whether to take this new job offer in Frankfurt or stay with my current environmental policy role in Berlin. I've been in Berlin for six months now and just got Thor settled in at the dog park near our apartment.
That sounds like a difficult decision. What are the main differences between the two positions that are making you hesitate?

The Frankfurt job would be with the European Environment Agency and pays about 15% more, but it means leaving my team here who I've worked with for years. Plus I just joined a local hiking group that meets every weekend near the Grunewald forest.
The hiking group sounds like a great way to connect with people in your new city. How important is that community aspect to you compared to the career advancement?

It's pretty important actually. I've been using my drone to photograph the hiking trails for our group's Instagram, and I've gotten really into documenting the local wildlife. I'm not sure I want to give that up.
That's a unique hobby! Have you considered if Frankfurt would offer similar opportunities for outdoor photography and community involvement?

I looked into it briefly. Frankfurt has the Taunus mountains nearby, but I don't know anyone there yet. My sourdough starter is also thriving here - I've been sharing loaves with my neighbors and colleagues.

It sounds like you've built quite a nice life in Berlin in just six months. What would your colleagues in Frankfurt say about your work with the drone photography? Could that be a unique skill to bring to the new role?
Actually, that's a good point. I could pitch using drone photography for environmental impact assessments. I just hate the idea of uprooting Thor again - he's still getting used to our new apartment.
Pets definitely make moves more complicated. Have you talked to anyone who's made a similar career move about how they balanced the personal and professional aspects?

Not yet, but I should. I have until next Friday to decide, so I'm trying to weigh everything - the 15% raise, the new opportunities, but also the community I'm building here with Thor and my sourdough experiments.
